final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial photo of a Korean café-style spread featuring fashionable coffee and desserts, no people, styled for Instagram — then overlaid with hand-drawn white-pen annotations in a personal diary sketchnote style.

Style/medium: smartphone photography aesthetic, Korean café flat-lay or slight overhead angle, warm and airy tones, Instagram Stories editorial feel; annotation layer rendered as white hand-drawn ink lines — single-stroke, slightly uneven, rough and organic, mimicking a real white gel pen on photo
Composition: overhead or slight 45° angle, generous negative space left intentionally for annotation breathing room, items loosely arranged with natural café props (linen napkin, ceramic cup, small plate)
Subject: artisan coffee drink (latte art or iced coffee) and at least one dessert (e.g. financier, cream puff, or tart) on a minimal café table surface; no human figures
Scene/backdrop: Korean minimalist café table surface — pale wood or white marble, soft natural window light, no people
Lighting/mood: soft diffused natural daylight from one side, gentle shadows, warm and intimate mood
Color palette: warm cream and off-white as base, muted terracotta and dusty rose as accent, white annotation lines as graphic layer
Materials/textures: ceramic matte cup surface, glossy glaze on pastry, soft linen texture, condensation on iced glass if applicable
Text (verbatim): hand-drawn Japanese handwriting annotations overlaid on the photo — written in hiragana (70%) and simple kanji (30%), short diary-like murmurs in the following style and placement rules:
  • Drink → taste / temperature / feeling (e.g.: さっぱり、やさしい甘さ)
  • Food → texture / deliciousness (e.g.: しっとり、さいこう)
  • Space/atmosphere → mood (e.g.: ほっとする、いい時間)
  • Overall one-liner → (e.g.: しあわせすぎた〜)
  Annotation style: white pen, single-stroke rough lines tracing object outlines, arrows and dashed lines as eye guides, small decorative elements (steam wisps, tiny sparkles ✦, heart ♡, small emoticon faces) added sparingly
Constraints: annotations must feel hand-drawn and organic, not digital-clean; white ink lines only; text must be Japanese (hiragana + simple kanji); leave intentional white space — do not crowd the frame; no people, no English text in annotations
Avoid: overly polished digital vector annotations, English labels, crowded layout, harsh shadows, dark moody tones, people or hands in frame
